channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Boost Training Goodput: How Continuous Checkpointing Optimizes Reliability in Orbax and MaxText
item.description: The new continuous checkpointing feature in Orbax and MaxText improves model training reliability and performance by optimizing I/O bandwidth and reducing failure risks. This innovation addresses limitations of traditional fixed-frequency checkpointing methods.

item.title: ADK Go 1.0 Arrives!
item.description: The launch of ADK Go 1.0 transitions AI scripts to production-ready services, emphasizing observability and security with features like OpenTelemetry integration and a new plugin system. This release aims to improve safety and efficiency in AI operations.

item.title: Developer’s Guide to Building ADK Agents with Skills
item.description: The ADK SkillToolset introduces a modular architecture for AI agents, allowing them to load expertise on demand and reduce token usage. This innovative approach enables dynamic capability expansion during runtime.

item.title: Bring state-of-the-art agentic skills to the edge with Gemma 4
item.description: Google DeepMind's Gemma 4 launch features advanced open models for multi-step planning and on-device workflows, enhancing autonomous capabilities. The release supports over 140 languages and is designed for various hardware.

item.title: Supporting Google Account username change in your app
item.description: Google now allows U.S. users to change their @gmail.com usernames while preserving account data, impacting app developers who rely on email for identification. This change necessitates a shift to using 'subject ID' for user identification.

item.title: TorchTPU: Running PyTorch Natively on TPUs at Google Scale
item.description: TorchTPU is a new stack enabling high-performance PyTorch workloads on Google’s TPU infrastructure, aiming to simplify code changes and optimize distributed training. Future plans include reducing compilation overhead and enhancing support for dynamic shapes.

item.title: Australia threatens tech companies with 2.25 percent tax if they don’t pay publishers
item.description: Australia proposes a 2.25 percent tax on tech companies' revenue to ensure they support journalism, offering an alternative to direct payments to local media. This move could reshape the financial responsibilities of social media and search platforms.

item.title: ‘AI deflation’ comes to India’s tech services giants and puts downward pressure on revenue
item.description: AI is starting to impact revenue models for India's major tech services firms, leading to potential job cuts despite stable headcounts. This trend indicates a shift in how these companies operate amid increasing automation.

item.title: China blocks Zuck’s acquisition of AI outfit Manus
item.description: China has blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, forcing the company to reconsider its AI strategy. This decision highlights ongoing regulatory challenges for foreign tech companies in China.

item.title: My 5 favorite open source operating systems that aren't Linux
item.description: The article lists five notable open-source operating systems that are not based on Linux, showcasing diverse options for users interested in alternatives. It highlights both historical and innovative projects.

item.title: This hidden TV feature tracks your viewing - here's how to turn it off (no matter what brand)
item.description: Samsung, LG, and Sony TVs have a hidden feature that tracks viewing habits, raising privacy concerns. Users can disable this feature to protect their data.

item.title: 77% of IT managers say their AI agents are out of control - 5 ways to rein in yours
item.description: A survey reveals that 77% of IT managers believe their AI agents are out of control, leading to unsanctioned applications. The article suggests five strategies to manage AI effectively.

item.title: Some Musk v. Altman Jurors Don't Like Elon Musk
item.description: Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI is complicated by potential jurors' negative opinions about him, which could impact the trial's outcome.

item.title: Microsoft's GitHub shifts to metered AI billing amid cost crisis
item.description: Microsoft's GitHub is shifting to metered billing for its AI services, ending the unlimited access model. This change reflects the company's need to manage costs amid rising expenses.

item.title: Adaptive Ultrasound Imaging with Physics-Informed NV-Raw2Insights-US AI
item.description: The article discusses advancements in ultrasound imaging using a new AI approach called NV-Raw2Insights-US, which integrates physics-informed techniques.

item.title: Jury selection in Musk v. Altman: ‘People don’t like him’
item.description: During jury selection for Musk v. Altman, many jurors expressed negative feelings about Musk, which could affect the trial's dynamics.

item.title: Google is testing AI chatbot search for YouTube
item.description: Google is testing a new AI-driven search experience for YouTube that aims to make searching feel more conversational and interactive.

item.title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
item.description: Many enterprises face challenges in adopting AI due to inadequate data management, highlighting the need for a robust data infrastructure.

item.title: Cybersec is a thankless job: expanding workload and shrinking pay packet
item.description: A report shows that cybersecurity professionals are experiencing stagnant wages despite increasing workloads and responsibilities, highlighting a disparity in the industry.

item.title: Canva apologizes after its AI tool replaces ‘Palestine’ in designs
item.description: Canva faced backlash after its AI tool inadvertently replaced the word 'Palestine' in user designs, raising concerns about AI's impact on content integrity.

item.title: repowire 0.10.2
item.description: The release of repowire 0.10.2 introduces a mesh network that allows AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex to communicate effectively. This advancement enhances collaboration among AI systems, potentially improving coding efficiency.

item.title: DeepSeek cuts V4-Pro prices by 75% and slashes cache costs across its entire API to a tenth
item.description: DeepSeek has drastically reduced the prices of its V4-Pro API by 75%, challenging competitors like GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus. This move aims to attract users by offering lower costs until May 2026.

item.title: Elon Musk vs Sam Altman Trial Kicks Off: Jury Selection Begins in High-Stakes OpenAI Lawsuit
item.description: The trial between Elon Musk and Sam Altman regarding OpenAI has commenced, with jury selection underway. This case could significantly impact the AI industry and financial stakes involved.

item.title: AI-Maxxing Is Hotly Trending But Getting On People’s Nerves
item.description: The trend of 'AI-maxxing' is gaining popularity, where individuals increasingly rely on AI for various tasks. However, this obsession is causing frustration among some people.

item.title: llama-cpp-py-sync 0.8943
item.description: The llama-cpp-py-sync 0.8943 release provides auto-synchronized Python bindings for llama.cpp, enhancing compatibility and functionality for developers using CFFI ABI mode.

item.title: graqle 0.52.1
item.description: The graqle 0.52.1 update introduces architecture-aware reasoning for AI tools, enabling users to build knowledge graphs from codebases. This feature enhances dependency and impact analysis.

item.title: llm-notebook 1.3.71
item.description: The llm-notebook 1.3.71 offers a Karpathy-style LLM wiki that integrates with various coding sessions, allowing users to compile and access knowledge efficiently.

item.title: Dharma, Data and Deception: An LLM-Powered Rhetorical Analysis of Cow-Urine Health Claims on YouTube
item.description: A rhetorical analysis using LLMs examines health misinformation related to cow-urine claims on YouTube, highlighting the intersection of cultural beliefs and scientific information.

item.title: zh-ocr-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The zh-ocr-mcp has been added to PyPI, providing a server for converting documents to Markdown format, enhancing accessibility and usability for developers.

item.title: Elon Musk vs Sam Altman trial begins today: What's at stake, key allegations, and all you need to know
item.description: The trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI is officially underway, with Musk alleging that OpenAI has strayed from its original mission for profit, seeking $134 billion in damages.

item.title: BlueFocus Reports 2025 Results as AI-Driven Revenue Reaches USD 546.05 Million and Token Usage Surpasses One Trillion
item.description: BlueFocus reported a significant revenue of USD 10.07 billion for 2025, with AI-driven revenue reaching USD 546.05 million and token usage surpassing one trillion. This highlights the growing impact of AI in their business model.

item.title: synsci-delphi-proxy added to PyPI
item.description: The synsci-delphi-proxy has been added to PyPI, allowing standard input/output MCP clients to connect with a Delphi HTTP API. This enhances interoperability for developers working with these tools.

item.title: cafitac-hermit-agent 0.3.49
item.description: The cafitac-hermit-agent 0.3.49 has been released, enabling Claude Code or Codex to handle repetitive coding tasks using more cost-effective local models. This can streamline coding workflows.

item.title: cyllama-vulkan 0.2.14
item.description: Cyllama-vulkan 0.2.14 is a new Python library for local AI inference, supporting llama, whisper, and stable-diffusion models without dependencies. This simplifies the setup for developers.

item.title: pyg-nightly 2.8.0.dev20260427
item.description: The pyg-nightly 2.8.0.dev20260427 is a Graph Neural Network Library for PyTorch, providing tools for developers working on graph-based AI applications. This enhances the capabilities of PyTorch for specific use cases.

item.title: langchain-keeperhub 0.1.0
item.description: Langchain-keeperhub 0.1.0 has been introduced, offering a toolkit for reliable Web3 execution via KeeperHub. This supports developers in building decentralized applications.

item.title: cyllama-sycl 0.2.14
item.description: Cyllama-sycl 0.2.14 is another version of the cyllama library for local AI inference, similar to cyllama-vulkan, emphasizing ease of use for developers. This reinforces the library's versatility.

item.title: Microsoft Adds Policy to Let IT Admins Uninstall Copilot From Enterprise Windows 11 Devices
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a policy allowing IT administrators to uninstall the Copilot app from enterprise Windows 11 devices. This gives organizations more control over their software environment.

item.title: stryda-langgraph added to PyPI
item.description: The stryda-langgraph has been added to PyPI, enabling governance integration as a LangGraph node between LLM agents and tool executors. This enhances governance in AI workflows.

item.title: stryda-langchain added to PyPI
item.description: Stryda-langchain has been released, allowing Stryda governance to be integrated into LangChain agents. This facilitates better governance and management of AI tools.

item.title: stryda-langchain 0.1.0
item.description: Stryda governance has been integrated into a LangChain agent, allowing for enhanced functionality through BaseTool wrapping or callback attachments.

item.title: simp-protocol added to PyPI
item.description: The Standardized Intent Messaging Protocol has been added to PyPI, providing a communication framework for AI agents.

item.title: アメリカ国務省がDeepSeekなど中国AI企業による「技術窃盗」を警戒、各国に異例の通達
item.description: The US State Department has issued a global warning about alleged technology theft by Chinese AI firms, including DeepSeek.

item.title: socratic-nexus 0.4.0
item.description: Socratic Nexus 0.4.0 has been released as a universal LLM client compatible with various AI models, featuring integrations with popular frameworks.

item.title: 4 Excel AI Tools Tested and Microsoft Copilot Finished Last
item.description: A recent test of four AI tools for Excel revealed that Microsoft Copilot performed the worst in handling complex tasks.

item.title: Samsung Mobile Division Faces First-Ever Net Loss as AI-Driven Memory Costs Squeeze Smartphone Margins
item.description: Samsung's mobile division is projected to face its first net loss due to rising AI-driven memory costs affecting smartphone margins.

item.title: The cost math behind routing Claude Code through Ollama (~90% cut)
item.description: A method to route Claude Code through Ollama has been developed, potentially reducing costs by approximately 90%.

item.title: The iOS 26.5 Fix Every iPhone User Has Been Waiting For
item.description: The upcoming iOS 26.5 update promises performance enhancements, better battery life, and new customization features for iPhone users.

item.title: Revolutionary AI breakthrough: Brain-inspired nanoelectronics slash energy consumption
item.description: Researchers have created a brain-inspired memristor that reduces AI energy consumption by up to 70%, merging memory and processing.

item.title: The AI Paradox: Decentralized revolution vs. technocratic tyranny
item.description: The book 'The AI Paradox' explores the dual nature of AI development, emphasizing the tension between decentralized empowerment and centralized control.
